DynamicToken.description:
Dynamic token - untyped JSON data.

Use when you need flexibility and don't want compile-time type checking. Rhai scripts can manipulate these freely.
DynamicToken.title: DynamicToken

EffectError.description:
Error token produced by the engine when an effect handler fails.

Constructed in `firing.rs` for every effect transition `_error` port. The `inputs` field contains the original port inputs (dynamic), enabling retry logic to re-queue the original job via `err.inputs.job`.

inputs.description: Original port inputs (dynamic — shape depends on the transition).

transition_id.description: Transition ID where the failure occurred.

EffectError.title: EffectError

ExecutorCancelInput.description:
Input to the `executor_cancel` effect handler.

Cancels a running execution identified by its execution ID.
ExecutorCancelInput.title: ExecutorCancelInput

ExecutorEventSignal.description:
Event signal delivered by the ExecutorWatcher for mid-execution events.

Covers progress, artifact, metric, phase, output, and log events.

category.description: Event category (progress, artifact, metric, phase, output, log).

sequence.description: Monotonic sequence number within this execution.

ExecutorEventSignal.title: ExecutorEventSignal

ExecutorStatusSignal.description:
Status signal delivered by the ExecutorWatcher.

All executor lifecycle statuses (accepted, running, completed, failed, timed_out, cancelled) share this envelope. The `detail` field carries status-specific data (e.g., outputs for completed, error info for failed).

status.description: Status name (accepted, running, completed, failed, timed_out, cancelled).

ExecutorStatusSignal.title: ExecutorStatusSignal

ConfigRef.description:
Pointer to a static node-config blob in object storage. The executor's `FetchConfigHook` downloads from `storage_path` via the global `ArtifactStore` and replaces `ExecutionSpec.config` with the fetched JSON.

The storage path is opaque to the executor — it's whatever key the compiler chose at publish time (Mekhan uses `templates/{template_id}/v{version}/{node_id}/node-config.json`).

overlay.description:
Per-job overlay shallow-merged onto the fetched static config by `FetchConfigHook` (overlay keys win). Lets a caller keep the large, stable config (prompts, tool schemas) in object storage while shipping the slim, turn-varying parts inline on the token.

The agent loop uses this to carry the per-turn transcript plumbing on existing wire fields (so the engine needs no new typed field): it sets `history`/`pending` to `{{input:...}}` placeholders that `resolve_inputs` fills from staged inputs, and `_history_write_key` — the per-turn blob key the worker writes the new cumulative transcript to after the model responds.

storage_path.description: Storage key inside the global artifact bucket.

ExecutionSpec.description:
Open-ended execution spec. The `backend` field selects which backend handles this job; `config` carries backend-specific parameters as opaque JSON.

Domain-level declarations (inputs, outputs) live here because staging hooks need them regardless of backend.

`config_ref` exists to keep large static configs (LLM schemas, prompts, catalogue projections) out of the per-job NATS token. When `config_ref` is set, `FetchConfigHook` downloads the blob at the named storage key and writes it into `config` before staging proceeds — the backends still read from `config` and don't know which path it took.

config.description:
Backend-specific configuration (opaque to domain and worker layers). Each backend deserializes this into its own typed config.

When `config_ref` is `Some`, this field is populated by `FetchConfigHook` at staging time from the referenced storage blob (overwriting whatever was on the wire — typically `null` / empty object). When `config_ref` is `None`, this field is taken as-is — the inline path used by tests and one-off programmatic jobs.

config_ref.description: Reference to a static config blob in object storage. Compiler-emitted jobs always set this; the inline `config` field is left empty and populated by `FetchConfigHook` before staging.

inputs.description: Declared input files to stage before execution.

outputs.description: Declared output files expected after execution.

InputDeclaration.description: Declaration of an input file to be staged before execution.

name.description: Name of this input (used as filename in inputs_dir).

required.description: Whether this input is required (default true).

source.description: Where to get the input data.

oneOf.description:
Reference to a file in storage.

When `storage` is `Some`, the file is downloaded from that specific backend. When `storage` is `None`, the global `ArtifactStore` is used (backward-compatible).

storage.description: Per-input storage backend config. Supports `{{ secret:KEY }}` in credentials.

oneOf.description: Inline JSON value (written as JSON file).
Raw text content (written verbatim, no JSON serialization).

OutputDeclaration.description: Declaration of an expected output from an execution.

kind.description: Declared field kind (lowercase: "text", "number", "bool", "json", "textarea", "select", "file", "signature", "timestamp"). When the service-side compiler emits a typed output port, this carries the kind across the service/executor boundary so the runner can strict-validate the emitted value against the declaration. `None` means "no kind validation" (back-compat with pre-typed jobs + non-Python backends). Backend-side validation is opt-in via `PETRI_VALIDATE_SCHEMAS`; unknown kind strings skip validation.

path.description: Relative path within outputs_dir. None means the value is set via IPC.

required.description: Whether this output is required (default true).

upload_to.description: Upload this file output to a specific storage destination after execution. Supports `{{ secret:KEY }}` patterns in storage credentials.

OutputUploadConfig.description: Configuration for uploading an output file to storage after execution.

destination_path.description: Destination path within the storage backend. When omitted, defaults to `artifacts/{execution_id}/outputs/{output_name}`.

storage.description: Storage backend configuration for the upload destination.

RetryConfig.description:
Retry behaviour for transient storage errors (504, connection drops, etc.).

Wired into the OpenDAL `RetryLayer`, which only retries operations whose errors return `is_temporary() == true` — non-transient failures (404, 403, etc.) are not retried.

Defaults are chosen to absorb a typical Hetzner / S3-compatible 504 burst (~3 attempts spaced 200ms / 400ms+jitter / 800ms+jitter ≈ 2s total) without blowing past a Slurm sbatch's wall-clock budget.

max_attempts.description: Maximum retry attempts (excluding the initial attempt). Default 3.

max_delay_ms.description: Cap on backoff delay in milliseconds. Default 10_000 (10s).

min_delay_ms.description: Initial backoff delay in milliseconds. Default 200.

oneOf.description: SFTP / SSH file server (docs/32 §4.1). `endpoint` is the SSH endpoint (`ssh://host:port`); `credentials.access_key` is the username and `credentials.secret_key` is the inline PEM private key (written to a 0600 temp file at operator-build). `prefix` carries the base path.

StorageConfig.description:
Configuration for a storage backend.

Used both at the executor level (global artifact store) and at the per-input / per-output level for multi-backend staging.

# Environment variables (executor-level)

```text EXECUTOR_STORAGE_BACKEND=s3 EXECUTOR_STORAGE_ENDPOINT=https://s3.amazonaws.com EXECUTOR_STORAGE_BUCKET=my-bucket EXECUTOR_STORAGE_REGION=us-east-1 EXECUTOR_STORAGE_PREFIX=executor/ EXECUTOR_STORAGE_CREDENTIALS_ACCESS_KEY=AKIA... EXECUTOR_STORAGE_CREDENTIALS_SECRET_KEY=wJa... ```

# executor.toml

```toml [storage] backend = "s3" endpoint = "https://minio.internal:9000" bucket = "artifacts" region = "us-east-1" prefix = "executor/"

[storage.credentials] access_key = "minioadmin" secret_key = "minioadmin" ```

bucket.description: Bucket or container name (ignored for local).

endpoint.description: Endpoint URL. For S3: the S3 endpoint. For local: the root directory path.

resource_alias.description: Optional workspace resource binding (e.g. an `s3` resource). When set, the executor's file-ops backend overlays `endpoint`, `region`, `bucket`, and credentials from the resource envelope at run time — per-step inline values still win on a field-by-field basis. Empty or absent means "use the inline fields directly".

retry.description: Retry behaviour for transient storage errors. Applied via OpenDAL's `RetryLayer`, so only errors with `is_temporary() == true` are retried.

StorageCredentials.description:
Credentials for accessing a storage backend.

Loaded from environment variables (`EXECUTOR_STORAGE_CREDENTIALS_ACCESS_KEY`, `EXECUTOR_STORAGE_CREDENTIALS_SECRET_KEY`) or from the `[storage.credentials]` section in `executor.toml`.

Credential fields support `{{ secret:KEY }}` patterns that are resolved by the staging pipeline before use.

access_key.description: Access key (S3 access key ID, GCS HMAC key, Azure account name).

secret_key.description: Secret key (S3 secret access key, GCS HMAC secret, Azure account key).

ExecutorSubmitInput.description:
Input to the `executor_submit` effect handler.

Submit an execution job with a typed [`ExecutionSpec`] describing the work to perform (backend type, config, inputs, outputs). Lifecycle fields (`run`, `retries`, `max_retries`) are passed through for retry tracking.

execution_id.description: Engine-stamped execution id flowed in from the scheduler net (where the same id was passed to the dispatcher, e.g. as `EXECUTOR_TARGET_EXEC_ID` for sbatch). The executor submit handler reuses this id as the NATS subject suffix so a one-shot consumer in the dispatched process exact-matches its own message.

run.description: Execution attempt number (0-indexed, incremented on retry).

spec.description: Typed execution specification (backend, config, inputs, outputs).

ExecutorSubmitInput.title: ExecutorSubmitInput

ExecutorSubmitted.description:
Output from the `executor_submit` effect handler.

Contains all input fields plus the engine-assigned `execution_id` for tracking the execution through its lifecycle (accepted → running → completed/failed/timed_out/cancelled).

execution_id.description: Engine-assigned execution identifier for lifecycle tracking.

spec.description: Execution spec (echoed from input, needed for retry resubmission).

ExecutorSubmitted.title: ExecutorSubmitted

groups.name: Pace audio over a data channel

name: 42 · Live Audio Stream (paced data channel + ?follow=1 player)

places.name: Pace audio over a data channel - Control Emit Inbox
Pace audio over a data channel - Control Token
Pace audio over a data channel - Parked Data (write-once)
Pace audio over a data channel - Output
Pace audio over a data channel - Data Channel 'speech'
Pace audio over a data channel - Data Channel 'speech' Close (producer status)

logic.source: #{ dead: #{ job_id: err.inputs.job.job_id, run: err.inputs.job.run, reason: err.error, retries_exhausted: 0 } }
#{ log: evt }
#{ log: evt }
#{ log: evt }
#{ log: evt }
#{ log: evt }
#{ log: evt }
#{ out: job }
#{ out: job }
#{ out: job }
#{ dead: #{ job_id: err.inputs.job.job_id, run: err.inputs.job.run, reason: err.error, retries_exhausted: 0 } }
throw "automated step 'Pace audio over a data channel' failed and no error handler is wired"
#{ log: evt }

#{ f: #{ job_id: e.job_id, run: e.run, retries: e.retries, max_retries: e.max_retries, spec: e.spec, executor_namespace: e.executor_namespace, feed_chunks: e.feed_chunks, reason: "timed_out" }, log: #{ level: "error", source: "executor", message: "Automated step timed out", detail: #{ execution_id: e.execution_id, run: e.run, retries: e.retries } } }

let d = input; d.job_id = "streamer"; d.run = 0; d.retries = 0; d.max_retries = 3; let job_inputs = [#{"name": "main.py", "required": true, "source": #{"content": "# Pace the uploaded WAV out over the `speech` data channel in ~real time, so a
# live tap (`?follow=1`) can play it through Web Audio WHILE this step is still
# producing (docs/25).
#
# The audio arrives as a `file`-kind Start field, so `start.audio` is a FileRef;
# `aithericon.file(...).retrieve()` brokers the bytes to a local path. We read
# the raw 16-bit PCM and write it over the `speech` Data/Out channel in ~200 ms
# slices, sleeping ~200 ms between writes — so the whole episode lasts about as
# long as the audio. Nothing in the net consumes the channel; the bytes ride the
# out-of-band JetStream datastream, which the UI taps directly.

import time
import wave

import aithericon
from aithericon import open_output, set_output

CHUNK_MS = 200  # audio per write — also the pacing sleep, so it streams ~real-time

wav_path = aithericon.file(start.audio["key"]).retrieve()  # noqa: F821

with wave.open(wav_path, "rb") as w:
    assert w.getsampwidth() == 2, "expect 16-bit PCM WAV"
    rate = w.getframerate()
    channels = w.getnchannels()
    pcm = w.readframes(w.getnframes())

# The live player assumes mono L16; downmix if needed (stdlib, no deps).
if channels > 1:
    import audioop

    pcm = audioop.tomono(pcm, 2, 0.5, 0.5)
    channels = 1

frame_bytes = int(rate * CHUNK_MS / 1000) * 2  # samples-per-slice * 2 bytes (mono 16-bit)
sent = 0

with open_output("speech") as out:
    for i in range(0, len(pcm), frame_bytes):
        chunk = pcm[i : i + frame_bytes]
        out.write(chunk, content_type=f"audio/L16;rate={rate}")
        sent += len(chunk)
        time.sleep(CHUNK_MS / 1000.0)  # pace ~real-time so the tap is perceptibly live

set_output("bytes_streamed", sent)
set_output("sample_rate", rate)
set_output("seconds", round(len(pcm) / (rate * 2), 2))
", "type": "raw"}}]; job_inputs.push(#{ "name": "input.json", "source": #{ "type": "inline", "value": input } }); job_inputs.push(#{ "name": "start.json", "source": #{ "type": "inline", "value": d_start } });  d.spec = #{ "backend": "python", "inputs": job_inputs, "outputs": [#{"kind": "number", "name": "bytes_streamed", "required": false}, #{"kind": "number", "name": "sample_rate", "required": false}, #{"kind": "number", "name": "seconds", "required": false}], "config_ref": #{ "storage_path": "templates/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/v0/streamer/node-config.json" }, "stream_events": ["metric", "progress", "phase", "log"], "channels": [#{"element_kind": "binary", "name": "speech", "plane": "data", "transport": "jetstream"}] }; if input._executor_namespace != () { d.executor_namespace = input._executor_namespace; } else { d.executor_namespace = "executor-python-grp/default"; } #{ job: d }

transitions.name: Pace audio over a data channel - Prepare

logic.source: #{ job: #{ job_id: f.job_id, run: f.run + 1, retries: f.retries + 1, max_retries: f.max_retries, spec: f.spec, executor_namespace: f.executor_namespace, feed_chunks: f.feed_chunks } }
#{ out: job }
#{ out: job }
#{ out: job }

let __rv0 = (d_streamer.detail.outputs.seconds); let __rv1 = (d_streamer.detail.outputs.bytes_streamed); let __rv = #{ "seconds": __rv0, "bytes_streamed": __rv1 }; let __out = input; if "exit_code" in __out { } else { __out.exit_code = #{ ok: true, value: __rv }; } #{ result: __out }
#{ out: #{ job_id: dead.job_id, reason: if dead.reason != () { dead.reason } else { "dead_letter" } } }
let d = tok; #{ data: d, main: d }

transitions.name: Start - Park Inputs (fork: park data, forward token)

logic.source: let d = input; d._process_name = "Live Audio Stream"; #{ output: d }

transitions.name: Pace audio over a data channel - Control Emit

logic.source: throw "automated step 'Pace audio over a data channel' dead-lettered (infra failure) and no error handler is wired"

transitions.name: Pace audio over a data channel - Dead-letter (no handler — crash net)

logic.source: #{ output: done }

transitions.name: Pace audio over a data channel - To Output

logic.source: let d = tok; let c = #{}; for k in d.keys() { if k.starts_with("_") || k == "task_id" || k == "status" { c[k] = d[k]; } } #{ data: d, ctrl: c }

transitions.name: Pace audio over a data channel - Yield (park data, forward control)
